cns: Anxiety; Restlessness; Headache; Miosis begins (pupils start constricting)
cvs: Mild bradycardia begins; Blood pressure normal/slightly low
other: Lacrimation; Diaphoresis (sweating); Urinary urgency
odour: Garlic / kerosene odour (from formulation solvent — OPTIONAL; may not be present)
sample: Gastric lavage contents; Blood (RBC ChE baseline)

cns: MIOSIS (pinpoint pupils — hallmark); Confusion; Muscle fasciculations; Anxiety; Headache severe
cvs: Bradycardia (significant); Hypotension; QTc prolongation on ECG
other: SLUDGE syndrome: Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defecation, GI distress, Emesis; Muscle twitching
odour: Garlic / kerosene odour (if ingested formulation; OPTIONAL)
sample: Blood (RBC AChE — depressed); Gastric lavage; Urine (alkyl phosphate metabolites)

cns: Seizures (generalised); Coma (progressive); Miosis persists; Muscle paralysis begins
cvs: Severe bradycardia or tachycardia (nicotinic); Arrhythmia; Hypotension worsening
respiratory: Respiratory failure (bronchospasm + secretions + muscle paralysis); Cyanosis
other: Generalised weakness; Flaccid paralysis; Bladder/bowel incontinence; Coma deepens
odour: Garlic odour may persist in gastric contents (OPTIONAL)
sample: Blood (serial RBC AChE); Urine (GC-MS metabolites); Viscera if fatal

cns: Intermediate Syndrome (IMS): Proximal limb weakness; Cranial nerve palsies; Neck flexor weakness
cvs: Resolved if survived; Cardiac status stabilising
respiratory: Respiratory paralysis (IMS — can re-occur after seemingly recovered); Requires ventilator
other: IMS affects proximal limbs, neck flexors, cranial nerves; No miosis at this stage; Organophosphate-induced delayed neuropathy (OPIDN) possible weeks later
odour: None at this stage
sample: Blood (RBC AChE — recovery monitor); Urine (metabolites still detectable); EMG (nerve conduction)

cns: Anxiety; Restlessness; Headache; Dizziness
gi: Nausea; Vomiting begins; Epigastric burning; Abdominal pain
other: Garlic/fish odour strongly present at this phase
odour: GARLIC / DECAYING FISH odour (phosphine + diphosphines; OFTEN STRONGLY PRESENT — practically diagnostic)
sample: Gastric lavage (CRITICAL — early); Blood (phosphine) Silver nitrate paper test at bedside

cns: Severe headache; Confusion; Agitation; Restlessness worsening
cvs: SEVERE HYPOTENSION; Cardiac arrhythmias (multiple types); Sinus tachycardia → bradycardia
respiratory: Pulmonary oedema developing; Dyspnoea; Cough; Respiratory distress
gi: Profuse vomiting; Abdominal pain; Corrosive injury to GI mucosa
other: Metabolic acidosis (severe); Mg2+ falls (hypomagnesaemia); Lactic acidosis; Diaphoresis
odour: GARLIC / FISH odour persists strongly in breath and gastric contents
sample: Gastric lavage; Blood (ABG — metabolic acidosis); Blood phosphine; Serum Mg2+; ECG monitoring

cns: Coma; Seizures (if hypoxia); Unresponsive
cvs: Cardiovascular collapse; Refractory hypotension; Multiple ECG changes (ST changes, QTc widening); Cardiac arrest
respiratory: Pulmonary oedema fully established; Respiratory failure; Cyanosis
other: Multiorgan failure; DIC (coagulation failure); Acute kidney injury; Hepatic dysfunction
odour: Odour may be diminishing as phosphine depletes; still detectable in gastric contents
sample: Post-mortem: Lung tissue (phosphine); Liver; Gastric contents; Blood phosphine; Histopathology

phase: Phase 1 — Acute Early (0–2 hrs, acute ingestion)
cns: Headache; Anxiety; No major CNS signs yet
gi: DOMINANT at this phase: Violent vomiting; Severe abdominal colic; Rice-water diarrhoea (classic — resembles cholera)
other: Garlic breath (acute); Metallic taste; Burning sensation in throat/oesophagus
odour: GARLIC / ONION odour (acute ingestion; OPTIONAL — may be faint)
sample: Gastric lavage contents; Urine (arsenic — acute); Blood

cns: Encephalopathy begins; Confusion; Delirium; Seizures (severe cases); Headache worsening
gi: Haemorrhagic gastroenteritis; Blood-stained rice-water diarrhoea; Dehydration
other: Peripheral neuropathy begins; Muscle weakness; Acute renal failure; Haemolysis
odour: Garlic odour in breath and gastric contents
sample: Blood (arsenic level); Urine (inorganic arsenic); Viscera if fatal; Gastric lavage

cns: Peripheral neuropathy (stocking-glove pattern); Encephalopathy; Memory impairment; Behavioural changes
gi: Abdominal pain; Nausea (recurrent); Anorexia; Weight loss
other: MEES' LINES on nails (transverse white bands; 1 line = 1 episode); Raindrop pigmentation on skin; Hyperpigmentation; Keratosis (palms/soles); Alopecia
odour: May be absent in chronic; or faint garlic odour on body/nails
route: Ingestion (chronic — repeatedly over time; homicidal pattern)
sample: HAIR (GOLD — 1 cm = 1 month); Nails; Urine; Blood (less useful chronic)

cns: Headache; Irritability; No major CNS at this phase
respiratory: Chemical pneumonitis if vapour inhaled; otherwise minimal
gi: Metallic taste; Nausea; Vomiting; Abdominal pain; Corrosive mouth/throat burns
other: Excessive salivation (ptyalism); Burning throat; Metallic taste (hallmark early sign)
odour: Metallic taste (subjective; not external odour; NO characteristic external odour)
route: Ingestion (inorganic — HgCl2); Inhalation (vapour — Hg0); Dermal (organic methyl mercury)
sample: Gastric lavage contents; Blood (mercury level); Urine

cns: CNS effects begin: Confusion; Tremors; Headache worsening; Anxiety
gi: Haemorrhagic colitis; Profuse bloody diarrhoea; Severe abdominal pain
other: Oliguria beginning; Renal tubular damage; Proteinuria; Acute kidney failure
odour: No characteristic external odour (mercury has none)
sample: Blood (Hg level); Urine (mercury excretion); Renal function tests

phase: Phase 3 — Chronic (weeks–months, methyl mercury / repeated exposure)
cns: ERETHISM (hallmark chronic Hg): Irritability; Shyness; Timidity; Memory loss; Insomnia; Depression; Ataxia; Tremors (intentional)
other: ACRODYNIA (pink disease) in children: Pink painful extremities; Swollen hands/feet; Hypertension; Diaphoresis; Tachycardia in children
odour: No external odour
sample: Hair (organic mercury; chronic exposure); Urine (inorganic chronic); Blood; Nails

phase: Phase 1 — Early (0–30 min, IV/IM route; or 30–90 min oral)
cns: Euphoria (initial); Analgesia; Drowsiness; Miosis begins
cvs: Mild bradycardia; Mild hypotension; Peripheral vasodilation
gi: Nausea; Vomiting (early — common); Decreased bowel sounds
other: Itching/flushing (histamine release); Warmth; Drowsiness
odour: NO characteristic odour (opium itself has earthy smell — usually not present in processed forms)
route: IV injection (heroin — fastest; 7–8 sec onset); Ingestion (morphine oral; 30–90 min); Inhalation (smoking)
sample: Blood (morphine level); Urine (immunoassay screen); Gastric lavage (oral)

cns: CLASSIC TRIAD: 1. Miosis (pinpoint — hallmark) 2. Decreased consciousness (stupor → coma) 3. Respiratory depression; Seizures rare; Hyporeflexia
cvs: Bradycardia; Hypotension; Peripheral vasodilation; Cool clammy skin
respiratory: RESPIRATORY DEPRESSION (primary cause of death); Slow rate <8/min; Shallow; Irregular; Cyanosis
gi: Vomiting (aspiration risk); Decreased bowel motility; Constipation
other: Hypothermia; Pale/cyanosed skin; Flaccid muscles; Needle track marks (IV); Naloxone reverses all signs
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (morphine/6-MAM); Urine (immunoassay); Gastric lavage (oral route)

cns: Deep coma; Absent reflexes; Brain death (anoxia)
other: Hypoxic brain injury; Rhabdomyolysis; Compartment syndrome (prolonged immobility)
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem blood (femoral — avoid cardiac); Vitreous humour; Hair (chronic use history); Liver

cns: Apprehension; Restlessness; Hyperacusis (sensitivity to sounds/stimuli); Stiffness begins
other: Heightened sensory sensitivity to light, noise, touch; Jaw stiffness
odour: INTENSELY BITTER taste (not external odour; subjective by victim; NO external odour)
sample: Gastric lavage contents (CRITICAL — early); Urine; Blood

cns: OPISTHOTONOS (arched back); GENERALISED TONIC CONVULSIONS (hallmark); PATIENT FULLY CONSCIOUS DURING SPASMS; Risus sardonicus (fixed grin — facial spasm); Hyperreflexia
cvs: Hypertension severe; Tachycardia; Risk of cardiac failure from spasm effort
respiratory: Respiratory muscle spasm → Apnoea during convulsions; Cyanosis
gi: Vomiting risk (aspiration danger); Abdominal muscle spasm
other: Spasms triggered by any sensory stimulus (light, sound, touch); Muscle hyperthermia; Rhabdomyolysis; Profuse sweating
odour: NO external odour
sample: Blood (strychnine level); Urine; Gastric lavage

cns: Death from exhaustion or asphyxia; Continuous spasms; No recovery between
respiratory: Respiratory arrest (from muscle rigidity); Terminal cyanosis
other: Hyperthermia (40°C+); Severe lactic acidosis; Renal failure (myoglobin); DIC
odour: NO external odour
sample: Post-mortem blood; Urine; Viscera (liver, kidney); Gastric contents

phase: Phase 1 — Hyper-acute (0–5 min — gas; 0–30 min — ingested salts)
cns: Headache; Dizziness; Anxiety; Confusion; Rapid loss of consciousness (HCN gas: seconds–2 min)
other: Flushed skin (early; histotoxic hypoxia); Lactic acidosis building
odour: BITTER ALMOND odour (HCN — classic; ~40–60% of population can detect it; OPTIONAL — genetic)
route: Inhalation (HCN gas — fastest); Ingestion (KCN/NaCN salts); Amygdalin (seeds — slower onset)
sample: Gastric lavage (ingestion); Blood (whole blood — cyanide unstable in serum)

cns: Seizures; Coma; Complete unconsciousness; Fixed dilated pupils (late)
cvs: BRIGHT RED VENOUS BLOOD (arterialised — hallmark); Cardiovascular collapse; Bradycardia → arrest; Hypotension
other: Lactic acidosis (severe); High anion gap; Bright red venous blood (venous pO2 high — oxygen not utilised); Cyanosis ABSENT (paradox — key sign)
odour: Bitter almond odour persists in breath, gastric contents, post-mortem tissues
sample: Whole blood (cyanide; NOT serum); Post-mortem blood; Gastric contents; Lung (if inhaled)

cns: Headache (frontal; hallmark early sign); Dizziness; Confusion; Nausea
other: Fatigue; Weakness; Visual disturbances; Cherry-red skin (may appear)
odour: NO odour — CO is COLOURLESS AND ODOURLESS (NOT detectable by smell; always absent)
route: Inhalation ONLY (vehicle exhaust; wood-burning fires; generators; coal stoves)
sample: Blood (COHb% by co-oximetry; urgent)

cns: Severe headache; Confusion; Disorientation; Impaired judgement; Ataxia; Syncope
respiratory: Dyspnoea at rest; Respiratory rate rises
other: Weakness; Collapse; Cherry-red skin more apparent; Visual disturbances
odour: NO odour — absolutely none at any stage
sample: Blood (COHb% — URGENT; co-oximetry only; normal blood gas pO2 can be falsely normal)

cns: Coma; Seizures; Brain damage; Unresponsive
other: CHERRY-RED post-mortem lividity (hallmark); Bright red blood (COHb — visually apparent at autopsy); Hypoxic brain injury; Rhabdomyolysis
odour: NO odour at any stage
sample: Post-mortem blood (COHb preserved post-mortem); Vitreous humour; Lung tissue; Brain

cns: Euphoria; Disinhibition; Mild incoordination; Slurred speech beginning
other: Impaired judgment; Decreased reaction time; Emotional lability
odour: SWEET / ALCOHOLIC odour (ethanol on breath; OFTEN PRESENT — easy to detect; OPTIONAL parameter)
sample: Blood (BAC — ethanol); Breath (breathalyser)

phase: Phase 2 — Acute Ethanol / Methanol latent (BAC >0.20% / 2–12 hrs methanol)
cns: Ataxia (gross); Confusion; Stupor; Nystagmus; Dysarthria; For METHANOL: visual disturbances begin (blurring, altered colour vision)
respiratory: Respiratory depression (ethanol, high BAC); Aspiration risk
other: Hypothermia; Hypoglycaemia; For methanol: no specific symptoms yet but formate accumulating
odour: STRONG ALCOHOLIC odour (ethanol); Methanol: minimal odour — cannot be detected by smell reliably
sample: Blood (BAC; methanol level); Urine (EtG if recent); Gastric lavage

phase: Phase 3 — Methanol toxic phase (12–48 hrs post-ingestion)
cns: VISUAL FAILURE (hallmark methanol): Blurred vision → Snowfield vision → BLINDNESS; Seizures (severe); Coma
respiratory: Kussmaul breathing (deep, rapid — severe metabolic acidosis); Respiratory failure
other: SEVERE METABOLIC ACIDOSIS (high anion gap); Formate accumulation; Optic nerve oedema; Retinal toxicity; Blindness permanent if untreated
odour: May have mild alcoholic odour; Methanol itself has faint odour — unreliable detection
sample: Blood (methanol level; formate level; ABG — acidosis); Urine; Vitreous humour (post-mortem)

cns: None significant
other: Local oropharyngeal irritation; Mild cervical swelling begins
odour: NO characteristic odour (hair dye may have chemical/ammonia smell if fresh — non-specific; OPTIONAL/unreliable)
route: Ingestion (suicidal — hair dye emulsion; 80 mL bowlful typical); Dermal (topical allergy — less severe)
sample: Gastric lavage contents (CRITICAL — early); Blood (baseline renal); Urine (baseline)

cns: None significant
cvs: Myocarditis developing; Cardiac conduction abnormalities; Fatal arrhythmia risk
respiratory: UPPER AIRWAY OBSTRUCTION (laryngeal/cervical oedema); Stridor; Dyspnoea; Emergency tracheostomy may be required
other: CERVICOFACIAL OEDEMA (HALLMARK — massive swelling of neck/face); Rhabdomyolysis begins; Dark/BROWN URINE (myoglobinuria — hallmark); Elevated CK
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Urine (BROWN — myoglobinuria; naked-eye diagnostic); Blood (CK, creatinine, cardiac enzymes); ECG

cns: None specific CNS
cvs: Myocarditis established; Arrhythmia; Cardiac failure risk
respiratory: Respiratory failure (if upper airway not secured); Pulmonary oedema
other: ACUTE RENAL FAILURE (myoglobin casts in renal tubules — ATN); Oliguria/Anuria; Elevated creatinine; Dialysis may be needed; Mortality 12–42%
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (creatinine; BUN; CK; LFTs; PPD level by HPLC); Urine; Viscera (post-mortem: renal histopath)

cns: None significant
gi: SEVERE CORROSIVE GI BURNS (hallmark early); Oral/oesophageal burns; Bloodstained vomit; Dysphagia; Nausea; Diarrhoea
other: Oropharyngeal ulcers; Corrosive injury; Oral pain; Dysphagia
odour: BLUE-GREEN liquid (commercial Gramoxone — visual sign at SCENE; not an odour); NO characteristic odour
route: Ingestion (suicidal — diluted Gramoxone; even small amounts fatal); Dermal (occupational; less common)
sample: Urine (dithionite test — bedside SPECIFIC; blue = positive); Blood plasma; Gastric lavage

cns: Confusion (hypoxia); Restlessness
respiratory: PULMONARY FIBROSIS BEGINS (hallmark); Progressive dyspnoea; Increasing O2 requirement; NOTE: supplemental O2 worsens lung injury
other: Acute renal failure; Hepatic dysfunction; Elevated liver enzymes; Raised creatinine
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (paraquat level; Proudfoot nomogram for prognosis); Urine (dithionite); Renal function

phase: Phase 3 — Late Fibrosis (3–14 days) — Highly lethal
cns: Confusion; Coma (hypoxia)
respiratory: PROGRESSIVE PULMONARY FIBROSIS FULLY ESTABLISHED; Respiratory failure; Ventilator-dependent; High FiO2 worsens; Death from hypoxic respiratory failure
other: Multiorgan failure; Acute renal failure; Hepatic failure; DIC; Death (high mortality even with treatment)
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Lung tissue (post-mortem; fibrosis on histopath); Blood (paraquat); Viscera

cns: CNS STIMULATION: Excitement; Tremors; Headache; Seizures (early — high dose)
gi: CORROSIVE BURNS: White coagulation of mucous membranes; Burning pain in mouth; Nausea; Vomiting; White/grey discolouration of gastric mucosa
other: Pallor of mucous membranes; Local coagulative necrosis; Skin burns (dermal)
odour: STRONG CARBOLIC / ANTISEPTIC ODOUR (unmistakable Dettol/ Lysol smell; OFTEN STRONGLY PRESENT; OPTIONAL parameter)
sample: Gastric lavage contents (CRITICAL — early); Blood; Urine (phenol metabolites early)

cns: CNS DEPRESSION (follows stimulation): Confusion; Drowsiness; Stupor; COMA; Hyporeflexia; Seizures → CNS depression transition
cvs: Hypotension (phase reversal from hypertension); Arrhythmia; Cardiac depression
gi: Haematemesis; GI perforation (severe); Continued corrosive injury
other: Dark brown/black urine (phenol metabolites; parahydroquinone); Skin: grey-white coagulation necrosis; Dehydration
odour: Carbolic odour persists in breath and gastric contents
sample: Urine (phenol metabolites; dark coloured — visual clue); Blood; Gastric lavage

cns: Deep coma; Brain injury (hypoxia/ toxic)
other: Acute renal failure; Methaemoglobinaemia (carbolic acid oxidises Hb); Cyanosis; Acidosis
odour: Carbolic odour may persist in post-mortem tissues
sample: Viscera (post-mortem); Urine (phenol); Blood (metHb level; GC-MS)

cns: Drowsiness; Slurred speech; Ataxia; Nystagmus; Impaired judgment
other: Hypothermia (mild); Reduced reflexes
odour: NO characteristic odour (tablets are odourless)
route: Ingestion (overdose; sedative abuse; often with alcohol)
sample: Blood (barbiturate level; serum); Gastric lavage; Urine (immunoassay)

cns: DEEP COMA; Absent reflexes; Fixed pupils (midbrain pressure); Loss of corneal reflex
respiratory: RESPIRATORY DEPRESSION (dose-dependent; Primary cause of death); Cheyne-Stokes breathing; Apnoea
other: Hypothermia (profound; hallmark severe barb poisoning); Bullous skin lesions (barb blisters — classic sign); Flaccid paralysis
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (serial barbiturate levels); Urine; Gastric lavage

cns: Brain death; No response; Isoelectric EEG
other: Profound hypothermia; Oliguria; Renal failure; Aspiration pneumonia; Barb blisters persist
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem blood (femoral vein); Brain tissue; Liver; Vitreous humour; Gastric contents

cns: Paraesthesia (tingling of tongue/lips — early); Headache; Dizziness; Restlessness; Hyperesthaesia
other: Fatigue; Tremors (fine, begins early); Anorexia; Petroleum solvent odour from formulation
odour: PETROLEUM / SOLVENT odour (from the formulation carrier — NOT the OCP itself; OPTIONAL/UNRELIABLE)
sample: Blood (OCP levels; serum — fat-soluble); Gastric lavage

cns: GENERALISED SEIZURES (HALLMARK — key distinction from OP; no miosis!); Tremors (coarse); Myoclonic jerks; Hyperactivity; Confusion
cvs: Hypertension severe; Cardiac arrhythmia (catecholamine sensitisation; arrhythmia if adrenaline given — DANGEROUS); Ventricular fibrillation
other: Seizures WITHOUT miosis (KEY — differentiates from OP poisoning); Hyperthermia; Rhabdomyolysis; Lactic acidosis
odour: Petroleum odour (if formulation present in gastric contents)
sample: Blood (OCP serum levels); Urine; Gastric lavage; Fat biopsy (chronic)

cns: Peripheral neuropathy; Cognitive impairment; Memory issues; Depression
other: Endocrine disruption (oestrogenic effects); Hepatic enzyme induction; Bioaccumulation in adipose tissue; Reproductive toxicity; Carcinogenicity (long-term)
odour: NO characteristic odour at chronic low levels
sample: Adipose tissue biopsy (GOLD for chronic OCP); Blood (serum lipids); Breast milk; Hair

cns: Headache; Dizziness; Restlessness; Anxiety
gi: NAUSEA; VOMITING (early and prominent); Abdominal pain; Epigastric burning
other: Garlic/phosphine odour strongly present; Diaphoresis
odour: GARLIC / PHOSPHINE odour (similar to AlP; OFTEN PRESENT — practically diagnostic; OPTIONAL parameter)
route: Ingestion (tablet/pellet — accidental or suicidal); Inhalation (phosphine gas released in stomach by gastric acid)
sample: Gastric lavage (CRITICAL); Blood (phosphine); Silver nitrate paper (bedside scene test)

cns: Confusion; Agitation; Headache worsening; Seizures (hypoxia)
cvs: Hypotension; Cardiac arrhythmia; ECG changes; Bradycardia
respiratory: Pulmonary oedema; Dyspnoea severe; Cough; Respiratory distress
gi: Profuse vomiting; Abdominal pain; Corrosive GI effects; Diarrhoea
other: Metabolic acidosis; Lactic acidosis; Liver dysfunction; Renal injury begins; Diaphoresis
odour: Garlic/phosphine odour persisting in breath and gastric contents
sample: Blood (phosphine; ABG — acidosis; ICP-MS for zinc); Gastric lavage; Liver enzymes; Renal function

cns: Coma; Unresponsive; Brain injury (hypoxia)
cvs: Cardiovascular collapse; Refractory hypotension; Cardiac arrest
respiratory: Respiratory failure; Apnoea; Cyanosis; Pulmonary oedema full
other: Multiorgan failure; Acute hepatic failure; Renal failure; DIC; Hypothermia; Zinc toxicity to liver
odour: Odour diminishing as phosphine depletes; still in gastric contents
sample: Post-mortem: Gastric contents; Lung tissue; Liver; Kidney; Blood (zinc by AAS/ICP-MS)

cns: Anxiety; Restlessness; Mild confusion; Disorientation begins; Euphoria (sometimes)
cvs: TACHYCARDIA (hallmark; sinus tachycardia; heart rate 100–160/min); Hypertension
gi: Nausea; Vomiting; Dry mouth (xerostomia; hallmark early sign); Dysphagia
other: MYDRIASIS (dilated pupils; hallmark — opposite of OP); Flushed/hot dry skin; Fever begins; Urinary retention; Decreased bowel sounds
odour: Sweet / musty odour from plant material (OPTIONAL — if seeds/ leaves present; NO odour from processed form)
route: Ingestion (seeds, leaves, tea, food adulteration — homicidal; accidental); Smoking (leaves); Dermal (eye exposure causes mydriasis)
sample: Gastric lavage (URGENT); Blood (atropine/scopolamine); Urine

phase: Phase 2 — Full Anticholinergic Syndrome (1–6 hrs) [Classic 'Mad as a Hen' phase]
cns: DELIRIUM (hallmark — patient appears psychotic); Visual HALLUCINATIONS (vivid, often colourful); Agitation; Combative behaviour; Amnesia (scopolamine); Confusion; Dysarthria; Bizarre behaviour; Seizures (severe cases)
respiratory: Tachypnoea; Decreased bronchosecretions (dry airways — opposite of OP bronchospasm)
gi: Dry mouth severe; Abdominal distension; Ileus; Constipation; Decreased bowel sounds
other: ANTICHOLINERGIC TOXIDROME: 'Blind as a bat' (mydriasis) 'Hot as a hare' (hyperthermia) 'Dry as a bone' (anhidrosis) 'Red as a beet' (flushing) 'Mad as a hen' (delirium); Urinary retention; Hyperthermia (>39°C)
odour: Plant/seed odour if present in vomit/ gastric contents (OPTIONAL)
route: Ingestion (seeds, leaves, homicidal food adulteration; tea infusion)
sample: Blood (atropine; scopolamine; hyoscyamine by LC-MS/MS); Gastric lavage; Urine

cns: Coma; Seizures; Respiratory depression (high dose); Brain injury (hypoxia)
cvs: Cardiovascular collapse (high dose); Arrhythmia; Cardiac arrest
respiratory: Respiratory failure (extreme doses); Aspiration risk (delirium + vomiting)
other: Hyperthermia severe; Rhabdomyolysis; Multiorgan failure; Death (rare but reported with seed ingestion)
odour: No significant odour at this stage
sample: Post-mortem: Vitreous humour (atropine/scopolamine detected; ScienceDirect 2016 case); Blood; Gastric contents; Liver; Urine

cns: Headache; Dizziness; Mild drowsiness; Anxiety
cvs: ECG changes may begin (PR interval prolongation); Bradycardia begins; Mild hypotension
gi: NAUSEA; VOMITING (prominent early; hallmark); Abdominal pain; Diarrhoea; Dysphagia (severe cases); Excessive salivation
other: Weakness; Lethargy; Visual disturbances (xanthopsia — yellow vision; classic for glycosides); Papaesthesia (tingling)
odour: NO characteristic odour (plant has mild smell but usually not detectable clinically)
route: Ingestion (leaves, seeds, bark — ALL parts toxic; even smoke from burning); Traditional medicine (abortifacient use India); Homicidal/suicidal
sample: Gastric lavage (URGENT); Blood (oleandrin by LC-MS/MS); ECG monitoring; Serum K+

cns: Confusion; Worsening drowsiness; Syncope (from cardiac failure)
cvs: CARDIAC ARRHYTHMIA (HALLMARK — multiple types): AV block (1st → complete); Bradycardia severe; Ventricular tachycardia; Ventricular fibrillation; HYPERKALAEMIA (K+ inhibition — fatal); Cardiac arrest
other: HYPERKALEMIA (K+ rise — Key biochemical marker; parallels severity); Xanthopsia (yellow vision); Weakness; Syncope; Cardiac failure signs
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (oleandrin by LC-MS/MS; cross-reacts with digoxin immunassay — use it for rapid screen); ECG (AV block type); Serum K+ (URGENT)

cns: Coma (cardiogenic cerebral hypoperfusion); Seizures (hypoxia)
cvs: Refractory arrhythmia; Complete heart block; Cardiovascular collapse; Sudden cardiac death
other: Acute renal failure (cardiorenal syndrome); Multiorgan congestion (post-mortem hallmark); Myocardial dysfunction on histopath
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem: Blood; Bile; Vitreous humour; Liver; Kidney; Gastric contents (LC-MS/MS for oleandrin)

cns: Anxiety; Headache; Drowsiness (scopolamine- like — krait); Restlessness
other: LOCAL effects (COBRA): Pain at bite site; Swelling; Fang marks (two puncture wounds); Local necrosis may begin; KRAIT: minimal local reaction — deceptively mild at this stage; Paraesthesia of limb
odour: NO characteristic odour (envenomation — odour not applicable)
route: Subcutaneous injection (snakebite — fang penetration); Route fixed: envenomation through fang puncture
sample: Blood (clotting time; CBC; coagulation profile); Wound assessment

phase: Phase 2 — Neurotoxic Phase (1–6 hrs) [Develops faster in Krait; slower in Cobra]
cns: NEUROMUSCULAR PARALYSIS (hallmark — descending): PTOSIS (drooping eyelids — FIRST sign of neurotoxicity); Ophthalmoplegia; Facial muscle weakness; Dysarthria; Dysphagia; KRAIT: Locked-in syndrome (reported N. India PMC 11892126)
respiratory: RESPIRATORY PARALYSIS (primary cause of death); Decreasing tidal volume; Cyanosis; Apnoea
other: Generalised flaccid paralysis (descending); Absent deep tendon reflexes; KRAIT bites often at night — victim wakes with paralysis; NEOSTIGMINE test can partially reverse (postsynaptic toxin — cobra responds better)
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (clotting time; ASV response monitor); Pulse oximetry; Arterial blood gas (respiratory failure); ECG (cobra cardiotoxin)

cns: Complete unresponsive paralysis; Coma (hypoxia); Brain injury
respiratory: Complete respiratory arrest; Apnoea; Ventilator required
other: Death from respiratory failure if untreated; ASV effective if given early; Neostigmine partially reversible (cobra); Post-mortem: venom detectable in wound tissue and blood by ELISA
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem: Wound tissue (venom ELISA — species-specific); Blood; Vitreous humour; Forensic: two fang marks on skin

cns: Anxiety; Headache; Drowsiness
other: SEVERE LOCAL EFFECTS (hallmark Viperidae): Intense pain at bite site; Rapid swelling/oedema (spreading); Tissue necrosis begins; Blistering; Fang marks with haemorrhage; Paraesthesia; Local bleeding from fang marks
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (20-min whole blood clotting time — WBCT20 — bedside; PT; aPTT; fibrinogen; D-dimer); Urine (haematuria check)

phase: Phase 2 — Systemic Haemotoxic Phase (2–12 hrs) [WBCT20 prolonged = KEY diagnostic]
cns: Confusion (hypotension); Restlessness; INTRACRANIAL BLEEDING (rare but fatal — 3% PMC 11892126)
other: COAGULOPATHY (HALLMARK): DIC (disseminated intravascular coagulation); Spontaneous bleeding from gums/nose/wounds; GUM BLEEDING (47%); HAEMATURIA (38%); SUBCONJUNCTIVAL haemorrhage (3%); ECCHYMOSIS; WBCT20 prolonged (non-clotting blood — bedside test); Acute Kidney Injury
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (WBCT20 — CRITICAL; PT; aPTT; fibrinogen; d-dimer; CBC; RFT); Urine (haematuria; RBC casts — renal); ECG

cns: Encephalopathy (uremia; intracranial bleed; hypotension); Coma (rare but reported)
gi: Hepatic failure; Jaundice; GI haemorrhage persisting
other: ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY (hallmark delayed Russell's viper): Oliguria; Anuria; Dialysis required; Tubular necrosis; Coagulopathy persists; Rhabdomyolysis (Russell's viper myotoxin)
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem: Blood; Wound tissue (venom ELISA); Kidney (histopath — ATN); Liver; Adrenal glands (viper adrenal haemorrhage — classic PM)

cns: Drowsiness (mild); Dizziness; Euphoria (brief, volatile hydrocarbon CNS effect)
respiratory: COUGH (immediate — primary presenting sign); Choking; Gagging; Aspiration — even small amounts during ingestion
gi: Nausea; Vomiting (DANGEROUS — aspiration risk during vomiting; NEVER induce vomiting); Burning in mouth/throat; Abdominal pain
other: Petroleum odour on breath and vomit (strong — nearly always present); Coughing; Gagging
odour: STRONG PETROLEUM/ KEROSENE odour on breath, vomit, gastric contents (OFTEN STRONGLY PRESENT; OPTIONAL parameter — but highly reliable)
route: Ingestion (accidental — children <5 years most common; stored in soft drink bottles India); Aspiration primary hazard (not GI toxicity)
sample: Chest X-ray (aspiration pneumonitis — urgent); Blood (SpO2); Gastric lavage (CONTRAINDICATED — risk of aspiration)

cns: CNS depression (hydrocarbon CNS effect); Ataxia; Seizures (hypoxia or direct CNS); Coma (severe cases)
cvs: Tachycardia; Arrhythmia (sensitisation to catecholamines — similar to OCP); Hypotension (severe)
respiratory: ASPIRATION PNEUMONITIS (HALLMARK): Fever; Tachypnoea; Bilateral crepitations; Cyanosis; Pulmonary infiltrates on CXR; Respiratory failure (ARDS pattern)
other: Fever (chemical pneumonitis); Cyanosis; Liver toxicity (rare; halogenated HC more); Bone marrow suppression (chronic benzene exposure)
odour: PETROLEUM odour persists in breath and gastric contents; Strong and persistent
sample: Chest X-ray (aspiration pneumonitis pattern); Arterial blood gas (hypoxia); Blood (CBC; LFTs; SpO2); Gastric contents (GC-HS for HC)

cns: Headache; Dizziness; Anxiety
gi: PROMINENT GI EFFECTS: Nausea; VOMITING (BLUE-GREEN vomit — hallmark and near- diagnostic visual sign); Abdominal pain; Diarrhoea; Metallic taste; Burning in throat
other: BLUE-GREEN vomit and gastric contents (copper sulphate); Metallic/astringent taste; Conjunctival icterus (yellowing — early haemolysis sign)
odour: NO specific external odour (metallic taste but not external odour)
route: Ingestion (suicidal — consuming dissolved blue crystals; accidental in food contamination; ritual use India); Dermal (industrial; skin burns)
sample: Gastric lavage (URGENT — before absorption); Blood (Cu level; haemolysis markers); Urine (haemoglobinuria)

cns: Confusion; Encephalopathy (copper toxicity + haemolysis + renal); Seizures (severe cases)
other: INTRAVASCULAR HAEMOLYSIS (hallmark copper toxicity): Haemoglobinuria (brown/red urine); Jaundice (icteric sclerae); Methaemoglobinaemia; Acute renal failure; Hepatic damage (copper accumulation); Methylene blue for metHb if needed
odour: NO significant external odour
sample: Blood (serum copper; LDH; haemolysis panel; CBC; LFTs); Urine (haemoglobin; copper); Blood (MetHb level)

cns: Coma; Encephalopathy (hepatic + copper direct CNS)
other: Acute renal failure (tubular damage; haemoglobin casts); Wilson-like acute copper toxicity; DIC; Multiorgan failure
odour: NO significant external odour
sample: Post-mortem: Blood; Liver (copper content by AAS/ICP-MS); Kidney; Gastric contents (blue-green material)

cns: Paraesthesia/tingling of mouth, tongue, face, extremities (IMMEDIATE hallmark — neurological start); Dizziness; Weakness; Anxiety
gi: Nausea; Vomiting; Abdominal pain; Diarrhoea; Hypersalivation
other: BURNING and tingling of lips/tongue/face (pathognomonic early sign of aconite); Paraesthesia spreading to limbs; Weakness begins; Increased secretions
odour: NO characteristic external odour
route: Ingestion (root/tuber; accidental — mixed with Ayurvedic prep; homicidal; food contamination); Traditional medicine (improperly processed aconite — Ayurveda)
sample: Gastric lavage (URGENT); Blood (aconitine by LC-MS/MS); Urine; ECG (urgent)

phase: Phase 2 — Cardiac/Paralytic (30 min–3 hrs) — RAPIDLY FATAL
cns: Motor weakness (ascending); Ataxia; Hyporeflexia; CNS depression (late)
cvs: CARDIAC ARRHYTHMIA (HALLMARK — most fatal feature): Ventricular tachycardia; Ventricular fibrillation; Bradycardia; Multiple ECG changes; Complete AV block; Cardiac arrest; Hypotension
respiratory: Respiratory muscle paralysis; Dyspnoea; Respiratory failure; Apnoea
other: GENERALISED PARALYSIS (ascending — from GI to limbs to respiratory); Hypotension (severe); Hypothermia; Diaphoresis; Death can occur within 1–6 hrs without treatment
odour: NO characteristic external odour
sample: Blood (aconitine; LC-MS/MS); ECG (continuous — arrhythmia detection); Blood (electrolytes); Urine

cns: Coma; Brain death (hypoxia)
cvs: Refractory ventricular fibrillation; Cardiac arrest; Cardiovascular collapse
other: Death from cardiac arrhythmia or respiratory paralysis — VERY rapid; Post-mortem: Aconitine detectable in tissues (stable PM); No specific gross PM findings — toxicology critical for diagnosis
odour: NO characteristic external odour
sample: Post-mortem: Blood; Gastric contents; Liver; Bile; Urine (if available); LC-MS/MS for aconitine (GC-MS also usable)

cns: Headache; Dizziness; Mild CNS effects
gi: SEVERE GI IRRITATION: Nausea; Vomiting; Diarrhoea; Abdominal pain; Burning in mouth/throat (corrosive latex)
other: OCULAR EFFECTS (if latex contacts eye): Severe conjunctivitis; Corneal ulceration; 'Milk latex' vesicles in mouth/skin; Dermal burns from latex; Local tissue necrosis
odour: Milky latex odour (if plant parts present; OPTIONAL — faint)
route: Ingestion (plant parts; leaves; latex; accidental/homicidal); Dermal (latex on skin; serious local burns); Ocular (latex in eye)
sample: Blood (calotropin by LC-MS/MS); Gastric lavage; ECG (cardiac glycoside monitoring)

cns: Confusion; Drowsiness; Weakness; CNS depression
cvs: CARDIAC ARRHYTHMIA (cardiac glycoside effect — similar to oleander/digoxin): Bradycardia; AV block; Ventricular arrhythmia; Hypotension; Hyperkalemia
other: HYPERKALEMIA; Weakness (generalised); Myocardial depression; Similar to digitalis toxicity; Hyperthermia (some reports)
odour: NO significant odour at this stage
sample: Blood (K+; ECG; calotropin by LC-MS/MS; cross-reacts with digoxin immunoassay); Serum electrolytes

cns: Drowsiness; Sedation; Anterograde amnesia (hallmark — cannot form new memories); Reduced anxiety; Mild confusion; Ataxia
other: Slurred speech; Ataxia (unsteady gait); Blurred vision; Muscle relaxation; Reduced coordination; Anterograde amnesia (very characteristic — victim cannot recall events after ingestion — forensic significance in DFSA)
odour: NO characteristic odour (tablets odourless; alcohol smell if combined ingestion)
route: Ingestion (overdose; abuse); Injection (IV diazepam); DFSA (Drug Facilitated Sexual Assault) — alcohol + BZD covertly added to drink
sample: Blood (BZD level — immunassay screen); Urine (up to 72 hrs; long-acting up to 7 days); Hair (DFSA investigations; detects back to 3–6 months)

phase: Phase 2 — Moderate–Severe (>2 hrs, high dose or combined with alcohol)
cns: DEEP SEDATION; Coma (BZD alone rarely causes deep coma unless combined with alcohol/opioids); Confusion; Hyporeflexia; Nystagmus
respiratory: Respiratory depression (SIGNIFICANT when combined with alcohol/ opioids — synergistic); Aspiration risk
other: Hypothermia (mild); Flaccid muscles; Flumazenil reversal (diagnostic and therapeutic — if BZD-only, reverses within 1–2 min); NEVER lethal alone (BZD therapeutic index very wide) — DANGER only with co-ingestion
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (BZD by GC-MS; immunassay positive is screening); Gastric lavage; Urine (BZD metabolites)

cns: Minimal or NO CNS symptoms (deceptive quiet phase — patient feels 'ok' but liver damage begins); Mild nausea; Headache
other: DECEPTIVE LATENT PHASE: Patient appears well; Liver damage (NAPQI toxic metabolite) begins silently; No clinical signs of hepatotoxicity yet; PARADOX: mildest phase = greatest need for treatment (N-acetylcysteine now is most effective)
odour: NO characteristic odour
route: Ingestion (overdose; suicidal; accidental paediatric); Available OTC India — very common
sample: Blood (paracetamol level — Rumack-Matthew nomogram; CRITICAL at 4 hrs); Liver function tests (AST/ALT — begin rising); INR

cns: Confusion (hepatic encephalopathy begins if severe); Restlessness
gi: RIGHT UPPER QUADRANT PAIN (liver tenderness — hallmark phase 2); Nausea; Vomiting; Jaundice begins; Hepatomegaly
other: HEPATOCELLULAR NECROSIS (primary toxicity): Jaundice (icteric sclerae); Coagulopathy (INR rises); Liver enzymes: AST/ALT >1000 IU/L (hepatic zone 3 necrosis); Acute renal failure (hepatorenal syndrome); Hypoglycaemia
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Blood (AST; ALT; INR; bilirubin; BUN; creatinine; glucose); Paracetamol level (if still early); Arterial blood gas

phase: Phase 3 — Fulminant Hepatic Failure (>72 hrs, severe cases)
cns: Hepatic encephalopathy (Grade III–IV); Coma; Cerebral oedema
gi: Fulminant liver failure; Deep jaundice; Ascites
other: FULMINANT HEPATIC FAILURE: Coagulopathy (INR >6); Hypoglycaemia (severe); Metabolic acidosis; Multiorgan failure; Cerebral oedema; Death (if no transplant available); Post-mortem: Centrilobular hepatic necrosis (zone 3 — pathognomonic histopath)
odour: NO characteristic odour
sample: Post-mortem: Liver (histopath — zone 3 centrilobular necrosis; diagnostic); Blood (paracetamol residual; LFTs; INR); Gastric contents
